title: Paul Tol's Colour Schemes
text: According Paul Tol’s technical note, bright, contrast, vibrant muted colour schemes colour-blind safe. light colour scheme reasonably distinct normal colour-blind vision intended fill labelled cells. pale dark schemes distinct either normal colour-blind vision used text background highlight cell table. qualitative colour schemes must used given (interpolation): colours picked maximum number supported values. Refer original document details recommended uses (see references).

title: Paul Tol's Colour Schemes
text: general rule, ordered data represented using rainbow scheme. three main arguments use (Tol 2021): spectral order visible light carries inherent magnitude message. bands almost constant hue sharp transitions , can perceived jumps data. Colour-blind people difficulty distinguishing colours rainbow. use avoided, Paul Tol’s technical note provides two colour schemes reasonably clear colour-blind vision. remain colour-blind safe, two schemes must comply following conditions: discrete rainbow scheme must interpolated. smooth rainbow scheme used full range (Tol 2021 suggests starting purple).  using smooth rainbow scheme: Start -white instead purple lowest data value occurs often; End red instead brown highest data value occurs often.
code: discrete_rainbow <- colour("discrete rainbow") plot_scheme(discrete_rainbow(14), colours = TRUE, size = 0.7) smooth_rainbow <- colour("smooth rainbow")  ## Start at purple instead of off-white plot(smooth_rainbow(256, range = c(0.25, 1))) ## End at red instead of brown plot(smooth_rainbow(256, range = c(0, 0.9)))

title: Colour Schemes for Scientific Data Visualization
text: Color blindness affects large number individuals. communicating scientific results colour palettes must therefore carefully chosen accessible readers. R package provides implementation Okabe Ito (2008), Tol (2021) Crameri (2018) colour schemes. schemes ready type data (qualitative, diverging sequential), colours distinct people, including colour-blind readers. package also provides tools simulate colour-blindness test well colours palette identifiable. simulate colour-blindness production-ready R figures may also interested colorblindr package. Tol (2021) Crameri (2018) offer carefully chosen schemes, ready type data, colours : Distinct people, including colour-blind readers, Distinct black white, Distinct screen paper, Matching well together, Citable reproducible. See vignette("tol") vignette("crameri") complete overview. specific uses, several scientific thematic schemes (geologic timescale, land cover, FAO soils, etc.) implemented, colour schemes may colour-blind safe. colour schemes implemented use base R ggplot2.

title: Color Palette — colour
text: palette character string giving name palette used (see ). reverse logical scalar: resulting vector colours reversed? names logical scalar: names colours kept resulting vector? lang character string specifying language colour names. must one "en" (English, default) "fr" (French). force logical scalar. TRUE, forces colour scheme interpolated. used routinely qualitative colour schemes, designed used remain colorblind-safe. ... arguments passed colorRampPalette.

title: Color Palette — colour
text: palette function following attributes, called single integer argument (number levels) returns (named) vector colors. palette character string giving name color scheme. type character string giving corresponding data type. One "qualitative", "diverging" "sequential". interpolate logical scalar: can color palette interpolated? missing character string giving hexadecimal representation color used NA values. max integer giving maximum number color values. relevant non-interpolated color schemes. colour schemes can interpolated (diverging sequential data), colour range can limited additional argument. range allows remove fraction colour domain (interpolated; see examples).

title: Color Palette — colour
text: following palettes available. maximum number supported colours brackets, value relevant qualitative colour schemes (divergent sequential schemes linearly interpolated). Qualitative data bright (7), high contrast (3), vibrant (7), muted (9), medium contrast (6), pale (6), dark (6), light (9). Diverging data sunset (11), BuRd (9), PRGn (9). Sequential data YlOrBr (9), iridescent (23), discrete rainbow (23), smooth rainbow (34).

title: Color Palette — colour
text: According Paul Tol's technical note, bright, highcontrast, vibrant muted colour schemes colourblind safe. mediumcontrast colour scheme designed situations needing colour pairs. light colour scheme reasonably distinct normal colourblind vision intended fill labeled cells. pale dark schemes distinct either normal colourblind vision used text background highlight cell table. Refer original document details recommended uses (see references).

title: Color Palette — colour

Colour-blind people difficulty distinguishing colours rainbow. use avoided, Paul Tol's technical note provides two colour schemes reasonably clear colour-blind vision. remain colour-blind safe, two schemes must comply following conditions: discreterainbow scheme must interpolated. smoothrainbow scheme used full range.

what: khroma: Colour Schemes for Scientific Data Visualization — khroma-package
title: khroma: Colour Schemes for Scientific Data Visualization — khroma-package
text: Colour schemes ready type data (qualitative, diverging sequential), colours distinct people, including colour-blind readers. package provides implementation Paul Tol (2018) Fabio Crameri (2018) <doi: 10.5194/gmd-11-2541-2018> colour schemes use 'graphics' 'ggplot2'. provides tools simulate colour-blindness test well colours palette identifiable. Several scientific thematic schemes (geologic timescale, land cover, FAO soils, etc.) also implemented.

title: khroma: Colour Schemes for Scientific Data Visualization — khroma-package

title: Plot Color Scheme — plot
text: plot() allows quickly display color scheme returned colour(). plot_scheme() shows colors plot. plot_map() plot_tiles() produce diagnostic map given color scheme. plot_scheme_colorblind() shows colors plot different types simulated color blindness.

title: Plot Color Scheme — plot
code: # S3 method for colour_scheme plot(x, ...)  plot_scheme(x, colours = FALSE, names = FALSE, size = 1)  plot_map(x)  plot_tiles(x, n = 512)  plot_scheme_colourblind(x)  plot_scheme_colorblind(x)

title: Plot Color Scheme — plot
text: x character vector colors. ... Currently used. colours logical scalar: hexadecimal representation colors displayed? names logical scalar: name colors displayed? size numeric value giving amount plotting text magnified relative default. Works cex parameter graphics::par(). n integer specifying size grid (defaults \(512\)).

title: Plot Color Scheme — plot
text: N. Frerebeau, V. Arel-Bundock
